events.title: UNPROMPTED: A Live Comedy Game Show
events.description: Love Make Some Noise, Game Changer, or Whose Line Is It Anyway? UNPROMPTED is your new Friday night chaos. Three comedians go head-to-head in a live improv game show packed with surprise prompts, ridiculous characters, and scenes made up on the spot. No scripts. No prep. Just fast choices and big swings. Audience judges decide each round as players battle through solo challenges, duo scenes, and full-group chaos for bragging rights and the coveted Golden Manatee. Tickets are $17.40 after taxes and fees. Doors open at 8:30 PM; show starts at 9:00 PM. Snacks, canned beer, and wine available! 7273296290

events.title: Cafe con Tampa
events.description: Tampa's Best of the Bay-winning Friday morning conversation that brings together the city's movers and shakers for a weekly presentation and Q&A. (813) 222-2233

events.title: 'The Skinny' on WMNF 88.5-FM
events.description: A nhour-long news radio show airing Fridays from 11 .m.-noon on WMNF Tampa 88.5-FM. Florida Phoenix reporter Mich Perry will be joined weekly by author and journalist Ben Montgomery plus CL’s Ray Roa, with appearances from guests who’ll discuss the news of the week.

events.title: FIFA World Cup Final Watch Party
events.description: Kickoff for the World Cup final is at 3 p.m. Centro Asturiano will open doors at 2:15 p.m. A cash bar will be available throughout the match.

events.title: Summer at The Dalí
events.description: This summer, The Dalí is offering kids and students a reduced Gallery Admission rate of $12—an invitation to spend a day surrounded by art that sparks curiosity, changes perspectives and and rewards a closer look. Whether you’re local, visiting with friends or planning a family day out, The Dalí is a cultural must-see. 7278233767

events.title: American Idiot
events.venue: David A. Straz Center - Jaeb Theater
events.description: What better way to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence? This two-time Tony Award-winning hit musical, based on the Grammy Award-winning multi-platinum album, boldly takes the American musical where it’s never gone before. This high-octane scorcher includes every song from Green Day’s album, American Idiot, as well as several songs from its follow-up release, 21st Century Breakdown. A high-concept rock opera, American Idiot explores disillusionment, consumerism, and the manipulation of the media and government through a trio of friends struggling to find meaning in a world they barely recognize. 813-229-7827

events.title: Summer Camp VBS Tropical Trek (FREE)
events.description: Join us for VBS Tropical Trek at Iglesia Tampa Bay, July 13th through 17th from 6:00 to 8:00 PM! Kids ages 4 to 11 are invited to enjoy a FREE week of games, music, Bible adventures, crafts, and tropical fun. Open to the entire community! Location: Iglesia Tampa Bay, 14214 North Nebraska Avenue in Tampa. Register today at: https://itb.s.gy/VBS2026 (813) 972-0444

events.title: GCP Summer One Acts 2026
events.description: Original short 10 minute plays chosen from hundreds of local & national submissions. Comedies, light dramas. Audience gets to vote for their favorites. 727- 322-0316

events.title: 7/17 Double Play at 1983 Tampa
events.description: Join us July 17th from 5pm-8pm at 1983 for Double Play Days — a family-friendly happy hour experience designed for fun both inside the game room and out on the turf. Throughout the month, guests can enjoy an energetic atmosphere filled with interactive games, kid-friendly activities, great food, and entertainment perfect for all ages. Parents can unwind while kids explore the arcade, challenge friends on the turf, and enjoy a rotating lineup of games and themed experiences that make every visit feel new. 8132513130

events.title: Historic Happy Hours at Hotel Flor
events.description: Step back into 1927 every Friday this month at The Dan in Hotel Flor! July marks the launch of Historic Happy Hours, a weekly event series kicking off a six-month centennial countdown toward the hotel's 100th anniversary. Each month spotlights a new era of the hotel's history, with its own theme, cocktails, and entertainment. July's theme, "The Start of an Era: Roaring in '27," celebrates the hotel's debut. Each Friday evening will feature live music, vintage cocktails, era appropriate décor, and specialty appetizers. Dress in 1920s attire for a free dessert! (813) 318-9719

events.title: Anime Matsuri On The Water: Anime Food & Music Fest
events.description: Anime Food & Music Fest Hosted by Mega Night Market Experience a vibrant celebration of anime and Asian culture featuring special guest voice actors Jeremy Inman and Tiffany Vollmer. Meet social media influencers and competitive eaters Raina Huang and Phuc Map, and catch an exclusive Q&A panel hosted by Trapped In Anime TV. The event features a dynamic lineup of live entertainment, including live performances and a DJ. Plus, explore a diverse marketplace packed with local anime merchandise vendors and authentic Asian street food vendors. Kids 12 & under FREE, General admission $10 727-692-0289

events.title: Easterseals School for Limitless Learning OPEN HOUSE
events.description: ⭐ Join Us for the Easterseals School for Limitless Learning Open House! ⭐ We are excited to welcome local parents, caregivers, and community members to our Open House on Friday, July 17, 2026, from 5:30 PM to 6:30 PM. This is a wonderful opportunity to tour our school, meet our team, learn about our programs, and see first-hand how we help children reach their fullest potential. To learn more, please email LimitlessLearning@esfl.org, call 813-236-5589. 8132365589

events.title: Good Trouble Lives On- Unity & Solidarity Mixer
events.description: Join us on Friday, July 17th as we come together to commemorate the incredible life, radical courage, and enduring legacy of former Congressman John Lewis. Change starts with community, and community starts with connection. We are bringing together local leaders, advocates, and neighbors for an evening of unity, reflection, and forward momentum. Come straight from work, bring a friend, and let’s keep the legacy alive. We kick off the night with networking, free appetizers, and drink specials. At 9 PM, DJ Sandman drops a classic 70s/80s/90s R&B soundtrack. Let’s connect, unite, and build momentum. See you there! (813) 657-1916

events.title: Battle in the Bay
events.description: Join Tampa Roller Derby for Battle of the Bay, a night packed with hard hits, fast jams, and nonstop derby action you won’t want to miss! Grab your crew, bring the energy, and get ready to choose your side in the Battle of the Bay! Game 1: Bradentucky Fusion Bombs vs Tampa Death Rollers Game 2: Bradentucky Nuclear Bombshells vs Tampa Sea Sirens Doors open: 6:30 PM First whistle: 7:00 PM Bring a chair for trackside seating Alcohol available for 21+ All tips support Tampa Roller Derby! 813-884-7688

events.title: Tampa Tarpons vs Bradenton Marauders
events.address: 3802 Dr Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Tampa

events.title: Florida American Indian Movement Benefit Show
events.description: “Join us as we raise money for our chapter and Missing/Murdered Indigenous Relatives. Learn about AIM, meet our allies, and see how you can get involved!” (727) 379-4677

events.title: Mean Girls Jr.
events.description: Featuring an extraordinary cast of talented young performers from across the Tampa Bay area, including award-winning singers, actors, dancers, and rising stars, this production delivers Broadway-style entertainment while encouraging meaningful conversations about friendship, respect, acceptance, and standing up for others. As audiences follow Cady Heron’s hilarious journey through the complicated social world of middle school, they will discover that the show’s biggest lesson isn’t about popularity—it’s about choosing kindness over cruelty and connection over division. Progressive Arts Theater’s commitment to inclusion extends far beyond the stage. 828-226-4290

events.title: Chico Bean
events.description: Hailing from Washington D.C. and known as ⅓ of the 85 South Show, Chico Bean knows how to deliver a well-executed joke. Born Anthony Bean, Chico Bean has the uncanny ability to memorize almost instantly the words to an old song or create a new quirky jingle on the spot. Chico contributed his skills to the long-running sketch-variety television show "Wild N' Out" hosted weekly by Nick Cannon and helped catapult the show to high viewership. Other credits include the Freestyle Funny Comedy Show and MTV 2's Guy Court. 813-864-4000

events.title: Pamela Jo Band
events.description: A great night to relax and enjoy singing and playing the "Old Time" music, a few originals and modern tunes. The genuine, Banjo-picking girl playing and singing her style of "Front Porch music." LIVE and IN PERSON. Pure acoustic music. (813) 575-9233

events.title: Move & Groove Yoga & Disco
events.venue: Lotus Pond Center For Yoga & Health
events.description: Move & Groove is a community movement night created to help you unwind, explore creativity, and connect with the simple joy of moving your body. Think disco ball vibes, silk ribbons and flow fans, good music, and a welcoming space to move without pressure or performance. This isn’t about how it looks...it’s about how it feels! We'll explore simple movement patterns and musicality together in a way that feels approachable and fun. No dance experience needed and no choreography to memorize. Move and Groove gives an opportunity to move, play, laugh, and maybe discover a new way of expressing yourself! 8139613160

events.title: Emo Social - Emo Night Party
events.description: Ft. Host Chris Kamrada (Ex-Dashboard Confessional, Falling In Reverse, There For Tomorrow) Emo Social has been throwing parties and shows for 5 years and has been everywhere from Florida, Hawaii, Texas, Chicago, NYC, Vegas, UK, Bali, and more. Featuring guests from Underoath, A Day To Remember, Mayday Parade, The Plot In You, Set It Off, Secondhand Serenade, Hellogoodbye, Wage War, and the list goes on! Doors & Music 8 PM to Close 21+ - $10; Under 21 - $15 All Ages - Under 18 Requires Parent or Guardian 9412014950

events.title: Schoolhouse Rock LIVE!
events.description: A pop culture phenomenon comes to the musical stage in "Schoolhouse Rock Live!" The Emmy Award-winning 1970s Saturday morning cartoon series that taught history, grammar, math, and more through clever, tuneful songs is not only making a small-screen comeback, but it’s also lighting up stages everywhere - from school multi-purpose rooms to university and regional theatres all around the country. URLs: Facebook: https://go.evvnt.com/3618437-3?pid=7176 Twitter: https://go.evvnt.com/3618437-4?pid=7176 Instagram: https://go.evvnt.com/3618437-5?pid=7176 YouTube: https://go.evvnt.com/3618437-6?pid=7176 Date and Time: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 20:00 - Fri, 17 Jul 2026 22:00 Venue details: Carrollwood Cultural Center, 4537 Lowell Road, Tampa, Florida, 33618, United States 813-922-8167

events.title: Soul Purpose IV
events.description: 7pc band out of Sarasota playing reggae and Ska music 🐬🐬🐬 we have Sarasota legend Richy Kicklighter on guitar Kevin Offitzer of Stick Figure on drums and Cody Moore of the Hip Abduction and old school AMI peeps might remember No Need 941-251-3930

events.title: Ghost Mysteries
events.description: Armed with ghost-hunting gear and quick wits, the world-famous Ghost Mysteries crew is ready to uncover strange noises, unexplained phenomena, and plenty of laughs. Join them as they investigate Tampa’s most haunted comedy theater: The Commodore. None

events.title: Mixed Media Night
events.description: Looking for art, music, and community in the Tampa Bay area? Mixed Media Night is connecting multiple genres of music and performances all in one evening! With talent from St. Pete, Tampa, Orlando, and Sarasota. New World Brewery Doors 7 pm $10 in advance and $15 at the door +1 (813) 304-0460

events.title: Broadway Miscast - The Sequel
events.description: Join us for the revival of last year's smash hit comedy musical revue as we present all-new Broadway hits, performed by great singers who absolutely do NOT “fit” the role as originally written. From start to finish, this high energy musical revue will surprise and delight you at every turn. 8132654000

events.title: Schoolhouse Rock Live!
events.description: School is in session! Based on the 1970s cartoon, this fast-paced musical teaches lessons with clever, catchy tunes. (813) 922-8167

events.title: Good Truoble Friday Afterparty w/ DJ Sandman
events.description: ATTENTION ALL 70s, 80s, & 90s BABIES! 🚨 Dust off your flyest gear and grab your crew, because this Friday night we are throwing it BACK! 🙌✨ We’re turning up the heat with a high-energy night packed with nothing but old-school hip hop and classic R&B. The legendary DJ Sandman will be on the decks spinning everything from Biggie to TLC, keeping you on the dance floor all night long (813) 657-1916

events.title: Special Guest DJ ESSE at M.Bird
events.description: Join us at M.Bird for an unforgettable night with special guest DJ ESSE, bringing the perfect soundtrack to Tampa’s skyline. Sip handcrafted cocktails, soak in the views, and dance under the stars as the beats take over the rooftop. Friday July 17th, starting at 10:00pm Book VIP tables and couches here or call 813-390-4017 Learn more on our website www.mbirdtampa.com 8132513130

events.title: Weekend Silent Meditation & Yogic Exercise Retreat In Person & Online
events.description: In the midst of busy lives filled with noise, distraction, and relentless activity, we rarely give the mind the space it needs to truly settle. This retreat offers a supported, gentle container in which to turn inward, reconnect with the breath, and touch the natural stillness that is always already present. Guided by Venerable Khenmo Sonam Choenzin, the retreat draws on the Anapanasati Sutra, the Buddha's foundational teaching on mindfulness of breathing, presented through the Drikung Kagyu Vajrayana tradition. Practice: Mindfulness of body, feelings, mind, and phenomena Meals will be provided. Registration is sliding-scale donation based. Accommodations available. 813-291-0974

events.title: Sunrise Sale
events.description: It's the 53rd Annual Sunrise Sale! 48 amazing and unique stores in downtown St Pete will open at sunrise - 6:43 AM on Thursday July 16. It's the biggest sale event of the year and deals are sizzling hot just like the temps. More reason to get out early and support small local businesses in your community. 7278733500

events.title: 53rd Annual Sunrise Sale
events.description: Join us on Thursday, July 16, as we open our doors bright and early at 6:43 AM for a special all-you-can-eat breakfast buffet. Enjoy a spread of all your morning favorites! All for just $10*. Looking to make the morning even better before hitting all the shops downtown St. Petersburg? Add our $6.95* Mimosas, Bellinis, and Bloody Marys. Score the best Sunrise Sale deal at The Birchwood. 🥂✨ *Pricing excludes tax and operations charge. 7273172848

events.title: MFA Shop Sunrise Sale 2026
events.description: The Museum of Fine Arts, St. Petersburg is pleased to participate in the 53d annual Sunrise Sale! Rise and shine… the MFA Shop will be open for business bright and early (6:43 am!) on July 16, 2026. Enjoy 20% off everything in the shop during this special event (MFA Members get an additional 10% bonus discount)! We have a unique selection of books, games, toys, jewelry, gifts, keepsakes, and more! 7278962667

events.title: Art After Dark
events.venue: Museum of Fine Arts St. Petersburg
events.description: Explore the galleries after hours, soak in the distinctive ambiance, and experience art in a fresh, captivating way—it’s the perfect night out for everyone to enjoy. Pair your visit with Café Clementine’s L’Apéro hours, featuring organic wines, local Green Bench beer, and a lineup of savory and sweet delights. Art After Dark occurs on select Thursday evenings throughout the year, with discounted admission, special programming, engaging docent-led tours, and more. 7278962667

events.title: Art on the West End | A Community Art & Vendor Market Benefiting Bess the Book Bus
events.description: ART ON THE WEST END A Community Art & Vendor Market Benefiting Bess the Book Bus Join us for an evening celebrating local art, small businesses, and community at The Bricks in historic Ybor City. Shop a curated selection of local artists, makers, and small businesses while enjoying happy hour specials, live music, great food, and drinks. A portion of food sales and raffle proceeds will benefit Bess the Book Bus, helping inspire a love of reading for children across the country. Thursday, July 16, 2026 5:00–8:00 PM The Bricks 1327 E. 7th Ave. Tampa, FL 33605 (813) 247-1785

events.title: Surreal Summer Nights At The Dalí
events.description: In celebration of the 250-year anniversary of the nation’s founding, join us for an indoor Independence Day party during this Surreal Summer Night. With half-off Gallery Admission after 5 p.m., explore our permanent collection of Dalí’s works, as well as our special exhibition, Dalí in America, escape the summer heat and experience an evening of art, music and celebration. 7278233767

events.title: Writer’s Workshop | First Draft Club
events.description: Curious about trying new poetry forms but not sure where to start? Join poet Ariana Matondo for First Draft Club, a welcoming workshop where experimentation is encouraged and perfection isn't the goal. Explore creative writing prompts and generative techniques, spend time writing and sharing, and leave with practical tools to continue your practice. Open to all experience levels—just bring your journal and an open mind. Your ticket also includes admission to Art After Dark, where you can explore the galleries and enjoy Café Clementine's L'Apéro hours before or after the workshop. 7278962667

events.title: Oh, Cuore Mia! opening reception
events.description: Oh, Cuore Mia (“my heart”) is a love letter to Ybor City, an exhibition at Tempus exploring the past, present, and future of a historic district in constant transformation. Artists reflect on and reinterpret Ybor narratives shaped by migration, labor, resistance, and reinvention in this group exhibition curated by Tempus Projects, with guest juror Jenn Ryann Miller. The artists reception takes place July 16 during Third Thursday at Kress Contemporary. Oh, Cuore Mia closes September 17, 2026, and will include additional programming (dates TBA) by Paul Wilborn, Jon Seals, and Palimpsest Book Studio. 813-340-9056

events.title: Bobby James
events.description: Talented singer song writer playing requests and all you favorite country songs! 941-251-3930

events.title: Tracheotomy with Breach of Peace, Drosera, and Katana
events.description: 7 PM Doors; 7:30 PM Show $15 Advance; $20 Day-Of-Show 9412014950

events.title: The Complete Meditation Toolkit - Part 1 The Basics (Thursday Meditation)
events.description: This series offers a practical guide to meditation that teaches us how to make ourself and others happy by developing inner peace. In this way, we find much more meaning in our lives. Each session will include teachings and guided meditations following The New Meditation Handbook. Each class is self-contained and is particularly suitable for those new to meditation. Everyone is welcome! "If you read this book with a good motivation free from negative views, I guarantee that you will receive great benefit for your daily happiness." - Geshe Kelsang Gyatso. 727-797-9770

events.title: Paint and Sip: Paint Night Done Right - Tampa
events.description: Turn your next night out into something creative with this paint and sip experience in Tampa! 800-385-0675

events.title: OPEN WIDE Open Mic
events.venue: The Ball: At The Base of Cocktail
events.description: Are you ready to OPEN WIDE? 👀🎤 Local musician, comedian, and actor Cat Lim is inviting performers of all talents to take the mic and showcase their creative expression. Sketch comedy? Singing? Tap Dancing? Stand-up? Anything goes at Open Wide! Strap in for a night full of laughs with Cat, who will be showcasing her own stand-up and sketch comedy skills (plus a sprinkling of musical moments) throughout the night! ✨🎤 Be sure to sign up in advance at tinyurl.com/openwidesignup (727) 258-7626

events.title: Chippendales - Talk Dirty to Me Tour
events.venue: Seminole Hard Rock Tampa Event Center

events.title: Happy Hour at The Dan
events.description: 🍸 Join us for Happy Hour at The Dan!🍸 Unwind in style every Sunday through Thursday from 4–6pm at The Dan at Hotel Flor! Whether you’re kicking off your evening early or winding down after a long day, our Happy Hour is the perfect excuse to gather your crew and settle in. Sip on crafted cocktails, enjoy great bites from our new Happy Hour food menu, and soak in the ambiance that makes The Dan unlike anywhere else. 🍸🍽️ 813-318-9719

events.title: Ken Ludwig's Dear Jack, Dear Louise
events.venue: David A. Straz Center for the Performing Arts, Shimberg Playhouse
events.description: U.S. Army Captain Jack Ludwig, a military doctor stationed in Oregon, begins writing to Louise Rabiner, an aspiring actress and dancer in New York City, hoping to meet her someday if the war will allow. But as the war continues, it threatens to end their relationship before it even starts. Tony Award-winning playwright Ken Ludwig (Baskerville: A Sherlock Holmes Mystery, Lend Me a Tenor) tells the joyous, heartwarming story of his parents’ courtship during World War II and the results are anything but expected. (813) 229-7827

events.title: KRESS CONTEMPORARY 3rd Thursday Open Studios
events.description: Experience Ybor City's creative community at KRESS CONTEMPORARY's Third Thursday Open Studios, held every third Thursday from 5–8 p.m. Explore the working studios of professional artists, meet the creators behind the work, and see original artwork up close in an inspiring, welcoming environment. Whether you're an art enthusiast, a first-time visitor, or a seasoned collector, you'll enjoy the opportunity to connect with artists and discover new work. Enjoy music, food, and conversation with Ybor's creative community. Occasional special programming includes artist talks, demonstrations, exhibitions, and other creative events. Admission is free, and everyone is welcome. 813-340-9056

events.title: Anything Goes open mic tuesdays
events.description: The Fringe's weekly open mic, open to all performance disciplines, with a fully operational theatre! Come and perform, or come and just watch! Bring tracks, videos, request lighting, and more. Let's cross pollinate our communities and create new things! Get fringey. Pay What You Can, 5 min limit. Perform and receive a free (non-alcoholic) drink. BYOB. 3473589682

events.title: HANDS DRAWING & PAINTING WORKSHOP
events.description: Adults & mature teens. They say that hands hold a language of their own, carrying so many bones, intricate joints, and complex structures. While they can often feel intimidating to capture on paper, they also offer an incredible opportunity to deepen your artistic practice. Over the course of this workshop, we are going to demystify the form, breaking down the structural foundations and anatomical complexities into manageable, beautiful shapes. Live models will help us learn to keep the hands in relationship with the rest of the body. Beginning, intermediate, advanced artists welcome. 813-417-5867

events.title: JOYLAND: Benaiah Cusack Art Exhibition
events.description: Joyland is a state of mind guided by wonder and curiosity through the creative process of art making. Inspired by imaginary coastal spaces, ocean light, rhythmic movement, tropical color, the energy of street art, and a hint of the cosmic, this body of work explores environments that feel both vibrant and introspective. Through Joyland, viewers are invited into a vivid world where imagination, energy and emotion converge—a space that rekindles wonder and joy. Benaiah Cusack is an abstract landscape and mixed-media painter working in acrylic, ink, graphite, and collage. 7278227872

events.title: Museums for All
events.description: Imagine Museum proudly participates in Museums for All — simply present your SNAP Benefit card and photo ID to one of our associates upon arrival and enjoy $3.00 admission to the museum. Children ages 6 and under are free. 7273001700

events.title: WORKSHOP OBJECT IN RELATIONSHIP DRAWING FOR KIDS
events.description: AGES 9-12 June 22-25, 2026 Students focus on the elements of picture making through drawing a still life. Beginning with the design of the arrangement, the student will choose the objects, create a composition and consider the lighting before then observing the many varied elements which are before them. Observational seeing will be developed as well as hand eye coordination through the use of the tools in the mark making process. ​There will be a lunch break at noon. Please provide lunch, snacks and drinks for your child each day. 813-417-5867

events.title: Universal Narratives: A Group Show Presented by Taylor Robenalt
events.description: Art has long been a powerful medium for storytelling, preserving history, and expressing personal and collective experiences. Ceramics, with its tactile and transformative qualities, provides a unique avenue for artists to shape narratives in a tangible form. This exhibition brings together artists from various backgrounds to create ceramic works telling compelling stories. Taylor Robenalt, Maria Albornoz, Bryan Wilkerson, Tammy Marinuzzi, Pavel Amromin, Casey Mcdonough, Robert Kolhouse, Chelsey Albert, Tiffany Leach, Keith Wallace Smith, Betsy Wild, Meghan Sullivan 7278227872

events.title: SUGAR HIGH: 2026 Members Show Art Exhibition
events.description: Sugar High, our annual members show, welcomes viewers to appreciate and recognize our artist members and celebrate their talent. The 2026 members exhibition presents nearly 150 works of art with the theme of excess and things we hate to love, as well as a joyful sense of color, chaos and childlike wonder. Thank you to our incredible member artists who have made this the most popular exhibition we do all year. 7278227872

events.title: Spirit of St.Pete, the Art of Alaina Greco, June 19-July 31
events.description: Returning artist Alaina Greco's Spirit of St. Pete show at PAW, June 19-July 31, is a "love letter to the wonderful intersection of city life and coastal wildness that makes St. Pete so special." Her art displays blends the familiar shapes of our favorite local landmarks with the playful, organic presence of the animals we share the peninsula with. Alaina also creates commission work of owner's furry family members. PAW is famous for it's award winning craft brews, pooch friendly atmosphere, great food truck fare, music, games, fun and ART! PAW is open Mondays-Thursdays 3-10, Fridays-Saturdays noon-midnight, Sundays 3-10 7272350970
